page: Amazon Web Services r8g.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory subsystem performance. The instance achieves a top-tier Passmark memory score, supported by strong uncached read and write memory bandwidths of 317.2 GB/sec and 130.27 GB/sec respectively. Compute performance is solid, with strong Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, alongside strong performance in Passmark database operations. However, the instance shows average performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and standard CPU tests, and exhibits weak to poor performance in medium and large LLM inference tasks.

The r8g.4xlarge does not include local NVMe storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Given its high memory density and strong database operation benchmarks, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, data analytics, and enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity without the need for local high-speed storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 128.0 GB of RAM, translating to 8.0 GB of memory per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier memory performance, highlighted by a strong Passmark memory score and high uncached memory bandwidth. Compute benchmarks show strong Geekbench single-core and multi-core results, though LLM inference performance remains average to poor. With an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, the r8g.4xlarge is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as databases, caching layers, and data processing applications that benefit from high memory density and efficient arm64 architecture.
